purpose: Define tenant/client-company profile, enabled modules, role options and login visibility without frontend changes or runtime activation.
principle: The consultora tenant/client profile is different from the end-client module used by the consultora to manage its own clients.

tyaInitialRecommendation.notes: For TyA initial operation, shopper/admin modules are operational priority. Client portal may be shown as sales preview if Paula decides.

loginRoleOptions.source: tenant module/role entitlements plus authenticated user claims
rules: Login should show only role/module options enabled for the tenant and allowed for the user.
Hidden modules must not appear as available actions.
Preview sales modules may appear as commercial teaser only if tenant setting allows it.
Security must not rely only on hidden UI; Auth claims and rules must enforce access later.

newLessons: Difference between tenant/company profile and end-client records
How to enable or hide modules by tenant

hardStops: Do not hard-code TyA module visibility in code.
Do not treat hidden UI as security boundary.
Do not mix tenant profile with consultora end-client records.
Do not write Firestore from this contract.
Do not activate Auth real from this contract.
